structural_description: The frame is divided into multiple zones, each containing a different equation related to force, mass, velocity, and acceleration. The equations are connected by lines and arrows, indicating their relationships.
layout: The frame is organized into a grid of nine zones, each containing a different equation. The equations are connected by lines and arrows, indicating their relationships.
text_readout:
ΣF/m = a
ΣF = ma
ΣF = mΔV/Δt
ΣF = Δ(mv)/Δt
ΣF = ΔP/Δt
m(v_f - v_i)/Δt
(mv_f - mv_i)/Δt
m(v_f - v_i)/Δt
spatial_map: The top left zone contains the equation ΣF/m = a, the top center zone contains ΣF = ma, the top right zone contains ΣF = mΔV/Δt, the middle left zone contains ΣF = Δ(mv)/Δt, the middle center zone contains ΣF = ΔP/Δt, the middle right zone contains ΣF = ΔP/Δt, the bottom left zone contains m(v_f - v_i)/Δt, the bottom center zone contains (mv_f - mv_i)/Δt, and the bottom right zone contains m(v_f - v_i)/Δt.
colors_styles: The equations are written in different colors: ΣF/m = a in yellow, ΣF = ma in blue, ΣF = mΔV/Δt in green, ΣF = Δ(mv)/Δt in white, ΣF = ΔP/Δt in red, m(v_f - v_i)/Δt in blue, (mv_f - mv_i)/Δt in green, and m(v_f - v_i)/Δt in blue. The lines and arrows are black.
